title: EA Internal: Precautionary Flood Warning Issued – Upper Usk Catchment
locationName: Powys | Brecon Beacons | Brecon and Radnorshire
evidence: Environment Agency issues precautionary flood warning for the upper River Usk catchment including Brecon and upper Abergavenny reaches. Gauge at Brecon showing rapid rise in response to overnight rainfall. EA operational teams placed on elevated monitoring. Warning published to Flood Warning service.

assessment.justification: EA flood warning for upper Usk is a credible early upstream signal. Gauge rise at Brecon consistent with observed rainfall in the catchment. Precautionary at this stage but hydrologically significant.
assessment.synthesis: EA precautionary flood warning for the upper Usk catchment. First EA signal of the event. Upstream indicator of developing conditions that will propagate downstream to Abergavenny and Newport.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Precautionary upstream warning. Conditions developing.

title: EA Internal: Flood Warning Active – Clydach Gorge and Heads of the Valleys
evidence: Environment Agency activates flood warning for the Clydach Gorge area following reports of flooding on the A465 and adjacent land. EA field team deployed to Brynmawr. Gauge data from Clydach tributary showing extreme flow rates. Corroborates Traffic Wales A465 closure.

assessment.justification: EA flood warning for Clydach Gorge corroborates Traffic Wales A465 closure. EA gauge data showing extreme flow rates adds hydrological authority to the event assessment. Field team deployment confirms severity.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood warning active for Clydach Gorge. Gauge data confirms extreme flow. Strongly corroborates A465 closure and adds hydrological evidence to the primary SE Wales impact.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. EA flood warning corroborates A465 closure. Gauge data authoritative.

title: EA Internal: Flood Alert Upgraded to Warning – River Usk at Abergavenny
evidence: Environment Agency upgrades flood alert to flood warning for the River Usk at Abergavenny. Gauge at Abergavenny Bridge at 3.8m and rising rapidly. EA officers attending property at risk in the town. Notification sent to properties on the Flood Warning Direct service.

assessment.justification: Flood warning upgrade at Abergavenny with gauge at 3.8m and rising. EA officer presence at at-risk properties confirms on-the-ground severity assessment. Direct notification to properties adds community dimension.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood warning upgraded at Abergavenny with gauge at 3.8m and rising. EA officers attending. Strongly corroborates Traffic Wales A40 advisory and community social media reports for Abergavenny.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Flood warning upgrade with gauge data and officer attendance. Strongly corroborated.

title: EA Internal: Flood Warning Active – River Usk at Newport
evidence: Environment Agency activates flood warning for the River Usk at Newport as gauge reaches 4.1m and continues to rise. EA staff deployed along the Newport floodplain. Warning covers residential properties in the Usk valley. Predicted peak level of 4.5m–4.8m by early evening.

assessment.justification: EA flood warning at Newport with gauge at 4.1m and rising. Predicted peak of 4.5m–4.8m represents a significant flood event for the Newport floodplain. EA staff deployment and predictive modelling adds authoritative assessment.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood warning active at Newport with River Usk at 4.1m and rising toward predicted peak of 4.5m–4.8m. EA staff deployed. Hydrological peak expected by early evening. Corroborates all concurrent official and community reporting from Newport.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Active flood warning with gauge data and hydrological peak prediction. High corroboration.

title: EA Internal: River Wye – Flood Warning Issued at Ross-on-Wye
evidence: Environment Agency issues flood warning for the River Wye at Ross-on-Wye as gauge reaches 4.2m and rising. A40 flooding risk elevated. EA field officers on site. Flood warning notification sent to approximately 120 registered properties in the Ross-on-Wye floodplain.

assessment.justification: EA flood warning at Ross-on-Wye is authoritative confirmation of the River Wye conditions reported by National Highways and the Hereford Times. Gauge at 4.2m is well above normal and rising trajectory is concerning for A40 and adjacent properties.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood warning at Ross-on-Wye confirms River Wye at 4.2m and rising. Corroborates National Highways A40 advisory and regional media reporting. 120 properties notified.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Official EA flood warning with gauge data. Strongly corroborates multiple concurrent sources.

title: EA Internal: Flood Alert Issued – River Severn at Gloucester
evidence: Environment Agency issues a flood alert (lower severity than warning) for the River Severn at Gloucester. Levels rising slowly. Alert is precautionary given the substantial volumes of water moving through the catchment from Wales and the Welsh Marches. Monitoring continues.

assessment.justification: EA flood alert for Gloucester Severn is a downstream propagation signal. The Severn receives flow from both the Wye and Usk catchments, both of which are in flood event. Precautionary at present but hydrologically consistent.
assessment.synthesis: EA flood alert for the Severn at Gloucester. Precautionary but hydrologically expected given the scale of the upstream Wye and Usk events. Downstream propagation signal. Corroborates Gloucestershire as a secondary impact area.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Precautionary downstream alert. Hydrologically consistent with upstream events.
